Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a POWL model with some anomalies Lets first understand the intended process flow and the actual model Intended flow 1 Receive Claim R 2 Assign Adjuster A 3 Evaluate Claim E 4 Approve Claim P 5 Notify Customer N 6 Close Claim C

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The actual POWL model Defines atomic activities for each step Creates a loop loop OperatorPOWL operator Operator LOOP children E P This means that the process will first execute E evaluate then either exit the loop or execute P approve and then E again

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the loop is intended to capture that after evaluation we may need to re approve and re evaluate until the loop is exited However note that in the loop the order is E then P but the intended flow has E then P then N then C The loop might cause multiple evaluations and approvals

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Creates an XOR xor OperatorPOWL operator Operator XOR children N skip This means that after the loop we either notify the customer or skip notification So in the model it is allowed to skip customer notification

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The root partial order R to A A occurs after R A to loop loop occurs after A loop to xor xor occurs after the loop A to C This edge allows closing the claim C to occur after A but without requiring that the loop and xor have completed

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is an anomaly because it allows C to occur before evaluation approval or notification

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the anomalies in the POWL model are 1 The loop between E and P This allows for multiple evaluations and approvals

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: In the intended process we expect one evaluation and one approval unless there are revisions but the loop structure might not be the intended way to handle that

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means that the loop can be exited after an evaluation without an approval In the intended flow after evaluation we always approve unless the claim is rejected but rejection is not modeled So the loop structure is ambiguous

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2 The XOR that allows skipping customer notification In the intended flow customer notification is a required step However the XOR allows skipping it

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3 The partial ordering that allows C close claim to occur after A assign adjuster without requiring the loop and xor to complete This means that we can close the claim immediately after assigning the adjuster without evaluation approval or notification This is a severe anomaly

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Now lets generate hypotheses for why these anomalies might exist 1 Changes in business rules Perhaps the business rule changed to allow closing a claim without full evaluation in certain cases eg if the claim is withdrawn However the model does not have a condition for that

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 2 Miscommunication The process modeler might have misunderstood the process and thought that closing the claim can happen at any time after assignment or that notification is optional in some cases

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 3 Technical errors The modeler might have intended to have a strict sequence but accidentally added an extra edge from A to C Also the loop and XOR might have been intended to represent decision points but were incorrectly structured

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 4 Inadequate constraints The tool used for modeling might not have enforced the required constraints so the modeler could create a partial order that does not enforce the intended sequence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now we need to propose how to verify these hypotheses using the database We have three tables claims adjusters and claim events We can write queries to check the actual event logs for each claim and see if the anomalies occur in practice

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets break down by anomaly Anomaly 1 Multiple evaluations and approvals loop structure We can check if there are claims that have more than one evaluation E or more than one approval P event Also note that in the loop the pattern is E to P to E until exit So we might see alternating E and P events

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly 2 Customer notification skipped We can check for claims that do not have a customer notification event N but have a close claim event C Alternatively check if there are claims that have an approval event P but no notification event N and are closed

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly 3 Premature closing We can check if there are claims that have a close claim event C that occurs before any evaluation E or approval P event Also check if close claim occurs immediately after assignment A without any other events

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Find claims with multiple evaluations or approvals

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Find claims that are closed without customer notification

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Find claims that are closed before evaluation or approval

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Find claims that have close event immediately after assignment without any other events in between

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also we can check the overall process by looking at the sequence of activities for each claim and see if they follow the intended pattern
